inline_comments: Twilio Voice API provider implementation. Uses Twilio Programmable Voice API with Media Streams for real-time bidirectional audio streaming. @see https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice @see https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/media-streams
Delete stored TwiML for a given `callId`. We keep TwiML in-memory only long enough to satisfy the initial Twilio webhook request (notify mode). Subsequent webhooks should not reuse it.
Delete stored TwiML for a call, addressed by Twilio's provider call SID. This is used when we only have `providerCallId` (e.g. hangup).
Clear TTS queue for a call (barge-in). Used when user starts speaking to interrupt current TTS playback.
Make an authenticated request to the Twilio API.

Verify Twilio webhook signature using HMAC-SHA1. Handles reverse proxy scenarios (Tailscale, nginx, ngrok) by reconstructing the public URL from forwarding headers. @see https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/webhooks/webhooks-security
Parse Twilio webhook event into normalized format.
For Twilio, we must return TwiML. Most actions are driven by Calls API updates,
so the webhook response is typically a pause to keep the call alive.
Parse Twilio direction to normalized format.
Convert Twilio webhook params to normalized event format.
Handle speech result (from <Gather>)
Handle DTMF
Handle call status changes
Generate TwiML response for webhook. When a call is answered, connects to media stream for bidirectional audio.
Get the WebSocket URL for media streaming. Derives from the public URL origin + stream path.
Extract just the origin (host) from the public URL, ignoring any path
Convert https:// to wss:// for WebSocket
Append the stream path
Generate TwiML to connect a call to a WebSocket media stream. This enables bidirectional audio streaming for real-time STT/TTS. @param streamUrl - WebSocket URL (wss://...) for the media stream
Extract token from URL and pass via <Parameter> instead of query string.
Twilio strips query params from WebSocket URLs, but delivers <Parameter>
values in the "start" message's customParameters field.
Initiate an outbound call via Twilio API. If inlineTwiml is provided, uses that directly (for notify mode). Otherwise, uses webhook URL for dynamic TwiML.
Create separate URL for status callbacks (required by Twilio)
Store TwiML content if provided (for notify mode)
We now serve it from the webhook endpoint instead of sending inline
Build request params - always use URL-based TwiML.
Twilio silently ignores `StatusCallback` when using the inline `Twiml` parameter.
Hang up a call via Twilio API.

Play TTS audio via Twilio. Two modes: 1. Core TTS + Media Streams: If TTS provider and media stream are available, generates audio via core TTS and streams it through WebSocket (preferred). 2. TwiML <Say>: Falls back to Twilio's native TTS with Polly voices. Note: This may not work on all Twilio accounts.
Try telephony TTS via media stream first (if configured)
Fall through to TwiML <Say> fallback
Fall back to TwiML <Say> (may not work on all accounts)
Play TTS via core TTS and Twilio Media Streams. Generates audio with core TTS, converts to mu-law, and streams via WebSocket. Uses a queue to serialize playback and prevent overlapping audio.
Stream audio in 20ms chunks (160 bytes at 8kHz mu-law)
Generate audio with core TTS (returns mu-law at 8kHz)
Pace the audio to match real-time playback
Send a mark to track when audio finishes
Start listening for speech via Twilio <Gather>.
Stop listening - for Twilio this is a no-op as <Gather> auto-ends.
Twilio's <Gather> automatically stops on speech end
No explicit action needed
Transient error — keep the call and rely on timer fallback
Twilio-specific types
